data.title: Great Lakes Dock and Dredge secures $114M U.S. Army contract
data.description:
Great Lakes Dock and Dredge (NASDAQ:GLDD) awarded a $114.98M firm-fixed-price contract for beach nourishment.

Bids were solicited via the internet with two received.

Work will be performed in Manasquan, New Jersey, with an estimated completion date of Nov. 17, 2025.

Fiscal 2025 civil construction funds in the amount of $114.98M were obligated at the time of the award.

U.S. Army Corps of Engineers is the contracting activity.

data.title: NRG Energy upsizes and prices senior notes
data.description:
• NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) priced its offering of $1,875M in aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes, consisting of (i) $925M 6.00% senior notes due 2033 and (ii) $950M 6.25% senior notes due 2034.
  • The aggregate principal amount of the offering was increased from the previously announced offering size of $1.5B.
  • The offering of the notes is expected to close on October 30, 2024.

data.title: Aperam to buy Universal Stainless for $45 per share
data.description:
Universal Stainless & Alloy Products (NASDAQ:USAP) will be acquired by Aperam in an all-cash transaction for $45.00 per share in cash, which represents a premium of ~19% to Universal’s (USAP) three-month average price.

Aperam is a global player in stainless, electrical and specialty steel and recycling, with customers in over 40 countries.

The transaction, which has been unanimously approved by the boards of directors of both companies, is expected to close in Q1 2025.

Following the closing of the transaction, Universal will continue to do business as Universal Stainless and maintain its headquarters in Bridgeville, PA.

“Becoming part of Aperam is also expected to benefit our customers because Aperam plans to invest in and support our manufacturing capabilities and technologies, enabling us to be more efficient and expand our product and services portfolio. This combination also gives us access to world-class research centers and innovations to further enhance our product quality and offerings.” said Christopher M. Zimmer, President and CEO of Universal (USAP).

Universal (USAP) has a market cap around $379M and the stock price close at $43.43 on Wednesday.

data.title: Sartorius Stedim Biotech S.A. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Sartorius Stedim Biotech S.A. (OTCPK:SDMHF) press release: Q3 GAAP EPS of €0.26.
  • Revenue of €655.4M (-1.7% Y/Y).
  • Outlook fiscal 2024: In terms of profitability, the underlying EBITDA margin is expected to reach 27 to 29 percent, with a slightly positive effect from the above-average profitability of the Polyplus business.

data.title: Asian markets mixed after China's housing plans underwhelms
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets were mixed on Thursday, with focus on Chinese housing markets and Japan's traded data.

Japan (NKY:IND) -0.48% Japan records higher than expected deficit, exports drop first time in 10 months

China (SHCOMP) +0.14% fluctuating around the flatline. China's housing ministry gears up to support struggling property sector. China announced in a media briefing today it will expand credit support to CNY 4 trillion to assist troubled property builders.

Hong Kong (HSI) +0.80%

Singapore’s non-oil domestic exports rose by 2.7% year-on-year in September 2024.

India (SENSEX) -0.43% down to an over-one-week low of 81,234 in early deals on Thursday, retreating for the 3rd straight day.

Australia (AS51) +0.86% Australia's unemployment rate steady at 4.1%

New Zealand's annual inflation fell to 2.2% in the third quarter from 3.3% in the previous quarter. The NZX 50 was flat, trading around 12,638 on Thursday after marking its worst day since February 2023 the day before.

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes moved higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley.

U.S. stock futures were moved lower on Thursday after the Dow and S&P 500 retreated from record highs in the previous session: Dow -0.11%; S&P 500 -20%; Nasdaq -0.20%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor GAAP EPS of $1.94 beats by $0.15, revenue of $23.5B beats by $210M
data.description:
• Taiwan Semiconductor press release (NYSE:TSM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.94 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $23.5B (+36.0% Y/Y) beats by $210M.
  • In the third quarter, shipments of 3-nanometer accounted for 20% of total wafer revenue; 5-nanometer accounted for 32%; 7-nanometer accounted for 17%. Advanced technologies, definedas 7-nanometer and more advanced technologies, accounted for 69% of total wafer revenue.

data.title: Japan records higher than expected deficit, exports drop first time in 10 months
data.description:
Japan’s trade balance shifted to a deficit of ¥294.24 billion in September, from a surplus of JPY 60.56 billion in the same month a year earlier.

It was the third straight month of a trade gap, coming in worse than the market forecast of a shortfall of ¥237.6 billion, with exports unexpectedly shrinking while imports grew.

Exports from Japan shrank by 1.7% Y/Y to ¥9,038.20 billion in September 2024, reversing a marginally revised 5.5% growth in August and missing market forecasts of a 0.5% rise. It was the first drop since November 2023.

Imports to Japan rose 2.1% Y/Y to ¥9,332.55 billion in September 2024 following a 2.3% growth in August, falling short of market consensus of 3.2%. It was the sixth month of increase but the softest in the sequence.

ETFs: EWJ, JEQ, DXJ, FXY, NKY:IND, USD:JPY, JPY:USD

data.title: Australia's unemployment rate steady at 4.1%
data.description:
• Australia’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate stood at 4.1% in September 2024, holding steady for the second straight month but falling short of market estimates of 4.2%.
  • The S&P/ASX 200 Index rallied 0.9% to around 8,360 on Thursday, hitting a new record high, with heavyweight bank stocks leading the market higher.
  • Australian shares also tracked gains on Wall Street overnight as strong corporate reports boosted investor confidence.
  • ETFs: (EWA), (FXA), (FLAU).

data.title: Nokia Non-GAAP EPS of €0.06, revenue of €4.34B
data.description:
• Nokia press release (NYSE:NOK): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.06.
  • Revenue of €4.34B (-7.9% Y/Y).
  • Order intake remained strong in Network Infrastructure, while the sales recovery continues to be slower than expected.▪ Comparable gross margin in Q3 increased by 490bps y-o-y to 45.7% (reported increased 500bps to 45.2%), withimprovements across business groups, particularly in Mobile Networks.▪ Q3 comparable operating margin increased 160bps y-o-y to 10.5% (reported up 70bps to 5.7%), mainly due to highergross margin, continued cost control and a benefit from the reversal of loss allowances for certain trade receivables.
  • Nokia's full year 2024 outlook is unchanged. Nokia currently expects comparable operating profit of between EUR 2.3billion and 2.9 billion and free cash flow conversion from comparable operating profit of between 30% and 60%
